title: Croton holodiscus
taxonomy: Croton holodiscus is classified under the family Euphorbiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the spurge family. This family is part of the order Malpighiales and the clade rosids. The order Malpighiales is a significant group within the larger clade of rosids, which includes a wide range of flowering plants. The clade rosids is a subclass of the larger group of flowering plants known as the eudicots. The class of flowering plants that Croton holodiscus belongs to is the Magnoliopsida.
uses: Croton holodiscus has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant has been reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. It is often used to treat skin conditions, wounds, and infections due to its antimicrobial properties. Additionally, it is used to alleviate fever, pain, and other symptoms associated with illnesses. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using Croton holodiscus for medicinal purposes.

side_effects: Croton holodiscus is not well-documented, but it may cause skin irritation and allergic reactions in some individuals due to its sap, which contains compounds that can be toxic to humans. Ingestion of the plant can also lead to gastrointestinal issues and other systemic problems. The sap may also cause skin discoloration and blisters in some cases.
